text: Something about repetition presses against my patience, or perhaps my anxiety. Five times okay, muttered—who is reassuring whom? The world slants around me, unfamiliarly dark and sterile, filled with stern glass and ambiguous shadows. Why must night in an office corridor evoke such existential unease? The second room reminds me in grand letters: R + D, research and development, the endless cycle of progress—yet that too is strangely silent, dim, empty. Am I okay? Who decides? My footsteps echo self-consciously. My mind feels suspended between apprehension and numb curiosity—a philosophical vertigo.
